title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · Relational Lens: Concurrency Control
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql concurrency control increment in a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare locking, multiversion, optimistic, and serialization-retry strategies from workload evidence.
Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Concurrency Control · Theory Concurrency Control
steps.instruction:
theory case — Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Concurrency Control contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Concurrency Control · Theory Concurrency Control.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare locking, multiversion, optimistic, and serialization-retry strategies from workload evidence. For Predict Concurrency Control · Theory Concurrency Control, keep Concurrency Control connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.

options.text: Retrying any failed transaction is always safe. That claim fails the predict concurrency control evidence boundary for Concurrency Control.

Run a convenient Concurrency Control query for predict concurrency control, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid. This does not satisfy Concurrency Control for a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol.
Compare locking, multiversion, optimistic, and serialization-retry strategies from workload evidence. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits. Apply that move specifically to Concurrency Control while producing a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol.
